commit.message:
Add Nx Release workflow documentation page (#1486)

This pull request updates the documentation to clarify and expand the
release automation options for monorepos, specifically by distinguishing
between Changesets and Nx-based release workflows. It introduces a new,
comprehensive guide for automating versioning and publishing with Nx,
and updates existing docs to direct Nx users to the appropriate
resource.

Resolves: CES-1451
depends-on: #1510

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: Azure Skills documentation generation pipeline (#365) (#367)

* .squad: Consolidation review orchestration complete

Merged 8 team member reviews of .NET project consolidation plan (42->40 projects, 6 actions approved, 1 deferred).

Changes:
- AD-027 through AD-034: Consolidation decisions, quality gates, safeguards
- Updated all 8 agent history files with consolidation review summaries
- Merged inbox decisions into decisions.md (inbox now empty)
- Created session log: .squad/log/2026-03-30T00-45-consolidation-review.md

Verdicts (all APPROVED WITH CONDITIONS except Quinn/Reeve who were fully approved):
- Riley: APPROVE WITH CHANGES - Action 7 rejected (subprocess isolation violation)
- Morgan: APPROVE WITH CHANGES - 7-hour estimate; behavioral caveat on Action 5
- Cameron: APPROVE WITH CHANGES - 3 quality gates required
- Quinn: APPROVED - No CI/CD changes needed
- Parker: APPROVE WITH CONTINGENCIES - 9 acceptance criteria
- Sage: RECOMMEND APPROVAL - 3 safeguards for Actions 2-4
- Reeve: APPROVED - 3 tech docs need targeted updates

Execution: 6-8 hours across 2 sprints (Phase 1-3). Quality gates mandatory per AD-028.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: Azure Skills documentation generation pipeline (#365)

Independent .NET pipeline in skills-generation/ that generates
customer-facing documentation for 24 Azure Skills in GitHub Copilot.

Architecture:
- skills-generation.slnx with SkillsGen.Core + SkillsGen.Cli + Tests
- 8-module pipeline: fetcher → skill-parser → trigger-parser →
  tier-assessor → template-filler + llm-rewriter → validator → orchestrator
- Hybrid generation: deterministic sections from parsed data,
  LLM-enhanced narrative with --no-llm fallback
- Handlebars template with structured prerequisites (RBAC, tools, resources)
- Acrolinx post-processor (static replacements, acronyms, contractions)
- Detailed per-skill + batch logging
- Generation manifest (JSON) for traceability

Key features:
- Tier assessment: 5-question deterministic decision tree (Tier 1/Tier 2)
- Skill identifier with links to source + triggers on GitHub
- 24 skills across 7 categories in skills-inventory.json
- start-azure-skills.sh entry point (independent from MCP pipeline)

Test coverage:
- 77 tests, 0 failures
- 81.6% line coverage, 73% branch coverage
- TDD per AD-007/AD-010

MCP pipeline: zero changes, zero regressions

Closes #365

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: Address code review findings from Copilot review

- Fix AzureOpenAiRewriter: bounds check on response.Content[0]
- Fix AzureOpenAiRewriter: remove duplicate systemPromptKnowledge assignment
- Fix AcrolinxPostProcessor: apply acronym expansions in reverse index order
- Fix GitHubSkillFetcher: add optional PAT token for authentication
- Fix LocalSkillFetcher: wrap file I/O in try-catch with helpful error messages

All 77 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: Address all squad review findings (3 blocking + 7 recommended)

Blocking fixes:
- Add exponential backoff retry to AzureOpenAiRewriter (5 retries, 1s-16s)
- Add skills-generation-ci.yml GitHub Actions workflow
- Add skills-generation/README.md + CHANGELOG.md entry

Recommended fixes:
- Fix Q5 tier assessor: check specific Azure service keywords, not always-true
- Add anti-hallucination rules to LLM system prompt
- Add empty response test for AzureOpenAiRewriter
- Log warnings on missing/invalid config files in AcrolinxPostProcessor
- Verify HttpClient not disposed in GitHubSkillFetcher (correct)
- Remove duplicate PREREQ_EXISTS validation gate
- Fix stopwatch bug in batch error path

Tests: 81 passed (+4 new), 0 failed
Line coverage: 81.1% | Branch: 72.1%

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: Clean parser output - decode entities, extract WHEN items, cap prompts

- Decode HTML entities and YAML escapes before extracting list items
- Extract WHEN: items into UseFor list for 'When to use' section
- Extract DO NOT USE WHEN: items into DoNotUseFor list
- Stop-pattern prevents DON'T USE items from leaking into WHEN list
- CleanListItem strips quotes, entities, ALL-CAPS internal markers
- Fall back to trigger prompts when UseFor is empty
- Cap example prompts at 10 maximum
- Use whatItProvides for 'What it provides' section with fallback
- Title consistency: metadata title = H1 = TOC entry (displayName)

106 tests pass, 23/24 skills generate cleanly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: NLP naturalization for UseFor/DoNotUseFor bullet points

Add NaturalizeItems() to SkillPageGenerator that converts raw keyword
fragments from SKILL.md into natural-language bullet points:

- Short noun phrases (< 5 words) get 'Work with' prefix
- Groups of 3-4 related short items merged: 'Work with X, Y, and Z'
- Verb-led items (create, deploy, configure, etc.) kept as-is
- Gerund forms recognized (creating, deploying, configuring)
- 5+ word phrases kept unchanged (already natural)
- Deduplication (case-insensitive)

Example: 'AI Search, query search, vector search, hybrid search'
  → 'Work with AI Search'
  → 'Query search'
  → 'Work with vector search, hybrid search, semantic search, and speech-to-text'

106 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: Address all 7 architectural review issues

HIGH:
1. Post-processor: reorder to WrapSkillNames→replacements→contractions→
   acronyms→URLs. Protect backticked content with placeholder extraction.
2. Parser: try-catch on all regex operations, return empty on parse failure.

MEDIUM:
3. Template: move '## When to use' heading inside {{#if hasUseFor}} block
   to prevent orphaned headings.
4. NLP: min length check, verb-form detection (-ing/-ed/-tion), 2-item
   comma fix ('X and Y' not 'X, and Y').
5. GitHub fetcher: retry with exponential backoff (3x, 1s/2s/4s) on
   429/403 rate limits. 100ms inter-skill delay in batch mode.
6. CLI: fail-fast on missing inventory/template files. Warn on optional
   Acrolinx/prompt files at startup.
7. Tier assessor: externalize threshold to constructor param (default 4).
   Expand Q5 Azure service keywords (+16 services).

106 tests pass, 0 failures.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: Rename azure-cost-optimization to azure-cost (matches upstream repo)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: ms.date format M/d/yyyy + fix Related content URLs

- ms.date: 2026-04-02 → 4/2/2026 (Learn requires M/D/YYYY)
- Related content links: /azure/copilot/ → /azure/developer/ (valid paths)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: Derive display names from slugs, clean ALL-CAPS, add --tests-path

- DeriveDisplayName: azure-deploy → Azure Deploy (with acronym handling)
- Inventory displayName overrides parser-derived name in batch mode
- CleanDescription downcases ALL-CAPS words (except known acronyms)
- LocalSkillFetcher: add --tests-path for separate tests directory
- Fix orphaned period in descriptions

106 tests pass, 24/24 skills generate.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: Review-2 fixes — lowercase ALL-CAPS, dedup acronyms, +19 tests

Code fixes:
- ALL-CAPS downcasing: Title Case → lowercase ('already-prepared' not 'Already-prepared')
- Remove conflicting orphaned-period regex (was adding space before .azure/)
- Dedup acronym expansions: 'Service (Service (AKS))' → 'Service (AKS)'

New tests (+19, total 125):
- 6 NaturalizeItems tests (empty, verb-led, short keywords, 2-item, long, single)
- 7 DeriveDisplayName theory cases + 3 CleanDescription behavioral tests
- 3 dual-path LocalSkillFetcher tests (testsPath, fallback, null)

24/24 skills regenerated cleanly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: Expand acronym preservation list (AWS, GCP, KQL, ADX, MSAL, +20 more)

Three sites had incomplete acronym lists causing aws/gcp/kql/msal to
appear lowercase in output. Unified all three to comprehensive set:
- CleanDescription keepUpper (parser)
- CleanListItem keepUpperInItems (parser)
- DeriveDisplayName acronyms (parser)

125 tests pass, 24/24 skills generate.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix Acrolinx issues: acronym casing, HTML entities, single-word bullets, shouldNotTrigger fallback

- Issue 1: Add FixAcronymCasing method to correct mis-cased acronyms (Ai→AI, Sdk→SDK, etc.) in naturalized bullet points
- Issue 2: Double-decode HTML entities in parser to handle double-encoded &quot; sequences
- Issue 3: Filter single-word verb/keyword items in NaturalizeItems (e.g., Recommend, Connect, Simulation)
- Issue 4: Stop using shouldNotTrigger test prompts as DoNotUseFor content — only use explicit SKILL.md items

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: Expand short bullets, fix acronyms, smart prereqs, rename Example triggers

- Short verb phrases (2-3 words) get ' in Azure' suffix for context
- FixAcronymCasing: Ai→AI, Aws→AWS, Gcp→GCP, etc. in naturalized items
- Remove 'When NOT to use' section (useless test data)
- Rename 'Example prompts' → 'Example triggers'
- Smart prereq detection from source file extensions (.ps1→PowerShell, .bicep→Bicep, .tf→Terraform, .js→Node.js)
- Resource detection from SKILL.md body content
- Double-decode HTML entities for azure-cost
- Remove single-word verb bullets (Recommend, Connect, etc.)

152 tests pass, 24/24 skills generate.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: Triple-curly Handlebars (no HTML escaping), earliest-cut description, dedup IgnoreCase

- Template: {{var}} → {{{var}}} to prevent " HTML encoding in markdown
- CleanDescription: find earliest marker across all patterns (was breaking on USE FOR before WHEN)
- Dedup regex: add IgnoreCase to both CleanDescription and AcrolinxPostProcessor
  Fixes 'Virtual machine scale set (virtual machine scale set (VMSS))' → '(VMSS)'

152 tests pass, 24/24 skills generate.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: Wrap technical terms in backticks, fix frontmatter branding, add Azure Ad variants

- WrapTechnicalTerms: eventhub, servicebus, DefaultAzureCredential, CopilotClient,
  createSession, sendAndWait, AMQP, BYOM, azd, dev/test → wrapped in backticks
- Static replacements now also applied to YAML frontmatter (was skipped)
- Added Azure Ad → Microsoft Entra ID, Entra id → Entra ID to replacements

152 tests pass, 24/24 skills generate.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: Add Vale CLI linting + fix remaining style issues

Pipeline integration:
- Vale CLI with Microsoft style guide for local pre-check
- .vale.ini configured: suppress false positives (FirstPerson in triggers,
  Dashes in bold text, Quotes in trigger examples, HeadingAcronyms)
- Vale binary in tools/ (gitignored)

Template fixes:
- Em-dash: ' — ' → '—' (no spaces) per Microsoft style
- 'Example triggers' section renamed from 'Example prompts'

Static replacement additions:
- backend → back end, frontend → front end
- the cloud → cloud
- auto-instrumentation → autoinstrumentation
- auto-upgrades → automatic upgrades
- Azure Ad → Microsoft Entra ID

Results: Vale 307→21 issues (93% reduction). 152 tests pass, 24/24 skills.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- NaturalizeItems: cap final list at 10 items max
- Filter items starting with How/What/Or/Show (leaked trigger prompts)
- azure-cost: 20 bullets → 10, cleaner and more focused

152 tests pass, 24/24 skills.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Built on the Citizen Intelligence Agency (CIA) platform, we provide systematic transparency through real-time analysis and 50+ years of historical data.

commit.message:
Align CMK/encryption articles with authoritative Q&A guidance

- Update FIPS 140-2 references to FIPS 140-3
- Remove CMK from AKV Standard use cases (Premium minimum)
- Remove retired services from CMK support list (LUIS, Personalizer, QnA Maker, MySQL Single Server, StorSimple)
- Add CMK control model framing to CMK support list intro
- Clarify key rotation only re-wraps DEKs, not re-encrypts data
- Add RSA-OAEP-256 wrapping algorithm recommendation
- Add KV Premium minimum and MHSM sovereignty guidance
- Clarify double encryption uses independent keys/operators/hierarchies
- Add security domain ownership and irrecoverable loss warning for MHSM
- Fix HSM typo (Model -> Module) in secrets-best-practices

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(map): add touch events to WorldHeatMap + ?country= param for pesquisa E2E

- WorldHeatMap: add onTouchStart/onTouchMove/onTouchEnd handlers mirroring
  mouse drag logic; 8px threshold for touch (vs 4px for mouse); touchAction:none
  on SVG so mobile browsers don't scroll page when panning/tapping the map
- pesquisa.tsx: support ?country=XX query param override so E2E and direct
  links can force a specific country regardless of IP geolocation
- e2e-smoke.ts: test /pesquisa?country=BR (BR has 27 paper-state rows in DB)
  instead of relying on server IP which geolocates to US/ES with no state data

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Rotation trading approach

- Lowered threshold to 60% (more signals)
- Shows up to 5 signals for rotation
- First signal marked 'TAKE NOW', others 'NEXT'
- Expected returns calculator
- Capital input at top for all signals
- ~7 trades/month, compound gains
- Based on 45% win rate, 2:1 R:R

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: persist CLI test results to shared store (#19)

goose test run now saves results to gooseapp/data/ — the same
store the dashboard uses. This means:
- CLI runs appear in dashboard history
- History is shared between CLI and UI
- Token usage, pass/fail, and full conversation are preserved

Implementation: CLI creates a TestRunStore and passes it to
run_tests(), which calls store.add_run() after each test.
The store parameter is optional to maintain backward compatibility.

Co-authored-by: AwesomeWorks Bot <copilot@awesomeworks.ai>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Remove EnableSingleFileAnalyzer workaround (SDK now handles it) (#66112)

The SDK now automatically enables EnableSingleFileAnalyzer when PublishAot=true
or IsAotCompatible=true (dotnet/sdk#31284). This manual workaround is no longer
needed.

Co-authored-by: danielfracea <235826647+danielfracea@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: Sprint 12 QA — Wizard API, confirm(), EntityTable actions, CLI await

Critical bug fixes found during QA review of Sprint 12:

Wizard API mismatch (pc-network, pc-alerts, pc-projects):
- Rewrote all wizard steps from { title, fields } to { label, render(data) }
- Wizard expects render functions returning HTML with data-field attrs
- Added initialData, validate functions, proper select rendering

confirm() signature (pc-lcm, pc-network, pc-alerts, pc-projects):
- Changed confirm('string') to confirm({ title, message, ... })
- confirm() destructures an object param, not a string

EntityTable actions (pc-lcm, pc-network, pc-alerts, pc-projects):
- Actions onClick receives single item from row menu, not array
- Wrapped single items in arrays: onClick: (item) => handler([item])

CLIService (alertCmd):
- Added async/await to state.update call in alert resolve

StateEngine seed data:
- Fixed proj-001 vpcs reference from 'Dev-VPC' to 'vpc-002' (UUID)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Separate per-client env config and rename "hosted" mode to "m365" (#8)

* Initial plan

* feat: separate env config per client, rename hosted to m365

- Split AppSettings into CoreSettings (core) + ApiSettings, ConsoleSettings, M365Settings (each package)
- Each client settings class reads from its own env file: .env.api, .env.console, .env.m365
- Created .env.api.example, .env.console.example, .env.m365.example; updated root .env.example to redirect
- Renamed AuthContext.hosted_user_token -> user_token; mode check now uses mode != "local"
- Renamed all hosted references to m365: create_hosted_app -> create_m365_app, _get_hosted_user_token -> _get_m365_user_token, _build_hosted_* -> _build_m365_*
- Changed auth_mode="hosted" to auth_mode="m365" in M365 app; auth_mode="api" in FastAPI app
- Renamed test_hosted_app.py -> test_m365_oauth_runtime.py with updated test names
- Updated README, docs/architecture.md, docs/m365-guide.md, docs/api-guide.md, docs/console-guide.md
- Updated .github/copilot-instructions.md and .github/instructions/ files
- All 107 tests pass, lint clean

commit.message:
Add KQL engine, query telemetry, DML commands, vector search, and Explorer enhancements

New features:
- KQL query engine with operators (where, project, extend, summarize, sort, top, take, distinct, count)
- Query telemetry store and activity tracking interfaces and storage implementations
- DML command service for INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE operations
- Vector embedding policy and vector index models
- KQL query controller and query telemetry controller in Host

Explorer (React):
- Container settings panel
- Import documents dialog
- KQL query editor component
- Query telemetry dashboard

Query engine improvements:
- Additional system functions and scalar subquery support
- Enhanced query explain service

Tests:
- KQL query executor tests
- DML command service, consistency middleware, indexing directive tests
- System function, scalar subquery, trigger execution, UDF query tests
- Vector search, unique key, query telemetry, TTL cleanup tests

feat: configurable storage backend with Explorer settings UI

Add support for selecting between SurrealDb, SQLite, and InMemory storage
backends via a side config file (emulator-config.json), CLI --storage
option, or the Explorer settings page.

Configuration:
- Add StorageType enum (SurrealDb, Sqlite, InMemory)
- Add Storage property to EmulatorOptions
- Load emulator-config.json from executable directory at startup
- Add --storage CLI option to the start command
- Priority: appsettings.json < emulator-config.json < CLI args

Storage backends:
- InMemoryDocumentStore: ConcurrentDictionary-based, ephemeral
- SqliteDocumentStore: Microsoft.Data.Sqlite, single-file persistence
- SqliteChangeFeedProvider: persistent change feed in SQLite
- InMemory/SQLite activity and query telemetry stores
- DocumentStoreHelpers: shared logic for partition keys, patches, etc.
- StorageServiceRegistration: DI dispatch based on StorageType

Explorer settings UI:
- Storage Configuration card with backend dropdown and data directory
- GET/PUT /api/emulator/config endpoint for emulator-config.json
- Restart-required messaging after saving storage changes
- Storage type shown in the Configuration info card

#34950 Cleanup nano refetch commands in the background (#42472)

<!-- Add the related story/sub-task/bug number, like Resolves #123, or
remove if NA -->
**Related issue:** Resolves #34950

I changed from the original spec of 100 old commands to 3 due to load
test results. Admittedly my load test meant a very large number of hosts
all checked in and triggered deletion at once but at 100 per host and
per command the load was too high. 3 still results in cleanup over time
and doesn't seem to cause load issues.

# Checklist for submitter

If some of the following don't apply, delete the relevant line.

- [x] Changes file added for user-visible changes in `changes/`,
`orbit/changes/` or `ee/fleetd-chrome/changes`.
See [Changes
files](https://github.com/fleetdm/fleet/blob/main/docs/Contributing/guides/committing-changes.md#changes-files)
for more information.

- [x] Input data is properly validated, `SELECT *` is avoided, SQL
injection is prevented (using placeholders for values in statements), JS
inline code is prevented especially for url redirects, and untrusted
data interpolated into shell scripts/commands is validated against shell
metacharacters.
- [x] If paths of existing endpoints are modified without backwards
compatibility, checked the frontend/CLI for any necessary changes

## Testing

- [x] Added/updated automated tests
- [x] Where appropriate, [automated tests simulate multiple hosts and
test for host
isolation](https://github.com/fleetdm/fleet/blob/main/docs/Contributing/reference/patterns-backend.md#unit-testing)
(updates to one hosts's records do not affect another)

- [x] QA'd all new/changed functionality manually

commit.message:
fix: correct SAML login sessions column names

COLS.samlLoginSessions had wrong columns (relay_state, request_id, expires_at)
that don't exist in the saml_login_sessions table. Fixed to match actual schema:
id, tenant_id, provider_id, attorney_id, name_id, name_id_format, session_index,
created_at, updated_at.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix scan latency in TOP50 mode when depth circuit breaker is active (#24)

* fix: reduce scan latency in TOP50 mode when depth/latency breaker is active

- In TOP50_FUTURES_ONLY mode, all 50 pairs are TIER1 so tier-based
  filtering was ineffective when the depth circuit breaker opened.
  Now limits scan to top 10 pairs by volume when breaker is active.
- Increase _MAX_CONCURRENT_SCANS from 15 to 20 for better parallelism.
- Increase DEPTH_CIRCUIT_BREAKER_THRESHOLD from 3 to 5 to prevent
  false trips from concurrent timeout cascades (10 inflight requests
  can all timeout from a brief network blip).
- Fix misleading log message to show actual restriction in TOP50 mode.

commit.message:
Fix depth timeout storms: window-based circuit breaker, higher timeout, always-on bookTicker pre-fetch (#23)

* Fix depth timeout storms: increase timeout to 5s, window-based circuit breaker, always pre-fetch bookTicker

- Increase _DEPTH_TIMEOUT_S from 3s to 5s to eliminate false timeouts
  when Binance responds in 3-4s under load
- Change circuit breaker from consecutive-count to time-window based
  tracking (30s window) so intermittent successes don't reset the counter
- Lower DEPTH_CIRCUIT_BREAKER_THRESHOLD from 5 to 3 for faster trips
- Always pre-fetch global bookTicker every cycle (not just when WS
  degraded) to seed Tier 2/3 spread cache and avoid individual depth calls
- Update tests to match new behavior

* fix(config): add base '/event2spec/' to vite.config.ts for GitHub Pages

instead of /event2spec/assets/..., causing 404 errors and a blank page.

commit.message:
fix: add missing channel entries to CHANNEL_SETUP_COMPATIBILITY, gate profiles, penalty weights, and inject order_book for OBI (#21)

Root causes:
1. CHANNEL_SETUP_COMPATIBILITY missing all 8 new scalp channels → channel_compatible always False
2. order_book not injected into smc_data before SCALP_OBI evaluate()
3. pair_profile and regime_context lost during per-channel SMC re-detection
4. Missing _CHANNEL_GATE_PROFILE and _CHANNEL_PENALTY_WEIGHTS for DIVERGENCE, SUPERTREND, ICHIMOKU, ORDERBLOCK
